page: Microsoft Azure D32_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes hyper-threading with 2 threads per core, 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations position the D32_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with notable strengths in specific memory and text-generation tasks. The Passmark memory score ranks in the top 25%, supported by a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1679.92 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 58.78 GB/sec. In CPU-bound tests, the instance achieved a Passmark CPU score of 36,815.64 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13,894.0. For language model workloads, the server demonstrates strong performance in text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 296.67 tokens/sec, though performance drops to 6.57 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32_v5 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, which is ideal for stateless applications or workloads relying on external network-attached storage. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid memory bandwidth, this instance is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale text processing or small-model machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.8 GHz, this Hyper-V managed instance features 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks place the D32_v5 in the average tier for most compute tasks, though it exhibits strong memory performance with a top-tier Passmark memory score and efficient small-model LLM text generation at 296.67 tokens/sec. The instance's balanced resource profile makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads without local storage requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, ideal for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring thirty-two vCPUs and high-bandwidth memory for enterprise applications and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Delivers strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% of Passmark benchmarks
Features no bundled local storage, decoupling compute from persistent storage costs
Suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and web serving
